profile.name: Haoxiang Liu / Harrison
profile.university: University of Science and Technology Beijing
profile.intro: I build AI-powered, embedded, and human-centered intelligent systems, with experience across multimodal agents, STM32 hardware development, smart home control, and speech rehabilitation products.

projects.title: Integrated STM32 Embedded Research & Education Platform
projects.description: I independently designed a custom STM32F103RBT6 learning board, covering hardware architecture, schematic design, PCB layout, SMT production, teaching materials, and course delivery.
highlights: Designed schematic and PCB independently in KiCad
Integrated GPIO, I2C, SPI, UART, PWM, ADC, timers, interrupts, sensors, displays, and IR communication
Led prototyping, SMT production, manufacturer communication, assembly tracking, and quality checks
Delivered 200+ boards to the university innovation center
Generated 30,000+ RMB in sales
Prepared 50,000+ words of technical documentation and PPT materials
Trained 1,000+ students

projects.title: AI-Powered Speech Assistant for Children with Speech Disorders
projects.role: Project Lead / Beijing Municipal Student Innovation Training Program
projects.description: A home-based speech rehabilitation system integrating a smart plush device, parent-side app, and cloud-based speech analysis model.
highlights: Designed software–hardware collaboration workflow
Fine-tuned Whisper with LoRA for atypical child speech
Used Praat to extract acoustic features for assessment
Built a loop covering collection, inference, evaluation, and feedback optimization
Won First Prize, North China Five Provinces Computer Application Competition
Won Second Prize, Beijing Computer Design Competition
Won Special Prize, USTB Cradle Cup Competition

projects.title: OpenClaw-based AI Smart Home Terminal
projects.role: Project Lead / Shenzhen Innox 2026 Geek Camp
projects.description: Nexus is an AI-agent-powered smart home terminal for multi-brand home environments.
highlights: Designed a natural-language-to-device-command workflow
Supported cross-brand device coordination and proactive automation
Added long-term memory and personalized preference learning
Presented the product as the main speaker during the roadshow
Won Champion in the Smart Home category of the Industry Track

projects.title: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents in Real-World Scenarios
projects.description: A benchmark for evaluating data visualization agents in realistic office scenarios.
highlights: Contributed to annotation guidelines for multi-turn intent alignment tasks
Identified ambiguity patterns in office visualization requests
Formalized clarification strategies through True Intent and Reaction Rules
Constructed ambiguous tasks and reviewed annotation consistency
Prepared experimental figures and supported result analysis
